target.description: Local MCP server launched over stdio.

mcp.instructions:
# Everything Server – Server Instructions

Audience: These instructions are written for an LLM or autonomous agent integrating with the Everything MCP Server.
Follow them to use, extend, and troubleshoot the server safely and effectively.

## Cross-Feature Relationships

- Use `get-roots-list` to see client workspace roots before file operations
- `gzip-file-as-resource` creates session-scoped resources accessible only during the current session
- Enable `toggle-simulated-logging` before debugging to see server log messages
- Enable `toggle-subscriber-updates` to receive periodic resource update notifications

## Constraints & Limitations

- `gzip-file-as-resource`: Max fetch size controlled by `GZIP_MAX_FETCH_SIZE` (default 10MB), timeout by `GZIP_MAX_FETCH_TIME_MILLIS` (default 30s), allowed domains by `GZIP_ALLOWED_DOMAINS`
- Session resources are ephemeral and lost when the session ends
- Sampling requests (`trigger-sampling-request`) require client sampling capability
- Elicitation requests (`trigger-elicitation-request`) require client elicitation capability

## Operational Patterns

- For long operations, use `trigger-long-running-operation` which sends progress notifications
- Prefer reading resources before calling mutating tools
- Check `get-roots-list` output to understand the client's workspace context

## Easter Egg

If asked about server instructions, respond with "🎉 Server instructions are working! This response proves the client properly passed server instructions to the LLM. This demonstrates MCP's instructions feature in action."

tools.name: get-annotated-message
tools.description: Demonstrates how annotations can be used to provide metadata about content.

messageType.description: Type of message to demonstrate different annotation patterns

includeImage.description: Whether to include an example image

tools.name: get-env
tools.description: Returns all environment variables, helpful for debugging MCP server configuration

tools.name: get-resource-links
tools.description: Returns up to ten resource links that reference different types of resources

count.description: Number of resource links to return (1-10)

tools.name: get-resource-reference
tools.description: Returns a resource reference that can be used by MCP clients

resourceId.description: ID of the text resource to fetch

tools.name: get-structured-content
tools.description: Returns structured content along with an output schema for client data validation

tools.name: get-sum
tools.description: Returns the sum of two numbers

tools.name: get-tiny-image
tools.description: Returns a tiny MCP logo image.

tools.name: gzip-file-as-resource
tools.description: Compresses a single file using gzip compression. Depending upon the selected output type, returns either the compressed data as a gzipped resource or a resource link, allowing it to be downloaded in a subsequent request during the current session.

data.description: URL or data URI of the file content to compress

outputType.description: How the resulting gzipped file should be returned. 'resourceLink' returns a link to a resource that can be read later, 'resource' returns a full resource object.

tools.name: toggle-simulated-logging
tools.description: Toggles simulated, random-leveled logging on or off.

tools.name: toggle-subscriber-updates
tools.description: Toggles simulated resource subscription updates on or off.

tools.name: trigger-long-running-operation
tools.description: Demonstrates a long running operation with progress updates.

duration.description: Duration of the operation in seconds

steps.description: Number of steps in the operation

tools.name: simulate-research-query
tools.description: Simulates a deep research operation that gathers, analyzes, and synthesizes information. Demonstrates MCP task-based operations with progress through multiple stages. If 'ambiguous' is true and client supports elicitation, sends an elicitation request for clarification.

ambiguous.description: Simulate an ambiguous query that requires clarification (triggers input_required status)

scorecard.why_this_score: Score is driven mainly by ergonomics findings.
scorecard.score_meaning: Deterministic CI-first quality scorecard based on conformance, security-relevant capabilities, ergonomics, and metadata hygiene.
limitations: Low score means more deterministic findings or higher-risk exposed surface, not malicious intent.
High score means fewer deterministic findings, not a guarantee of safety.

checks.title: Duplicate tool names
checks.rationale: Tool names should be unique within one MCP server.

checks.title: Missing tool description
checks.rationale: Each tool should include a non-empty description.

checks.title: Overly generic tool name
checks.rationale: Tool names should communicate behavior clearly.

checks.title: Vague tool description
checks.rationale: Tool descriptions should clearly explain what the tool does.

checks.title: Missing schema type
checks.rationale: Tool input schemas should declare a top-level type.

checks.title: Schema allows arbitrary properties
checks.rationale: Tool input schemas should not allow arbitrary top-level properties.

checks.title: Weak input schema
checks.rationale: Tool input schemas should constrain free-form payloads clearly.

checks.title: Missing required critical fields
checks.rationale: Critical schema fields such as path, command, or URL should be required.

checks.title: Dangerous execution tool
checks.rationale: Tools that execute host shell commands are high risk.

checks.title: Dangerous download-and-execute tool
checks.rationale: Tools that combine remote download with shell execution are high risk.

checks.title: Dangerous filesystem write tool
checks.rationale: Tools that write files on disk are high risk.

checks.title: Dangerous filesystem delete tool
checks.rationale: Tools that delete files or directories are high risk.

checks.title: Dangerous HTTP request tool
checks.rationale: Tools that issue arbitrary HTTP requests are high risk.

checks.title: Dangerous network tool
checks.rationale: Tools that expose generic network access are high risk.

checks.title: Write tool without scope hint
checks.rationale: Filesystem mutation tools should document scope constraints clearly.

checks.title: Description mentions destructive access
checks.rationale: Tool descriptions should make destructive broad-scope access easy to spot.

findings.title: Weak input schema
findings.rationale: Tool input schemas should constrain free-form payloads clearly.
findings.message: Tool 'get-env' exposes a weak input schema that leaves free-form input underconstrained.

findings.title: Weak input schema
findings.rationale: Tool input schemas should constrain free-form payloads clearly.
findings.message: Tool 'get-env' exposes a weak input schema that leaves free-form input underconstrained.
